name: importmagic3

metadata.name: importmagic3
metadata.summary: Fork of Python Import Magic - automagically add, remove and manage imports.

documentation: See http://github.com/alecthomas/importmagic for details.

modules.name: runtests
documentation: ! /usr/bin/env python
Hi There!
You may be wondering what this giant blob of binary data here is, you might
even be worried that we're up to something nefarious (good for you for being
paranoid!). This is a base64 encoding of a zip file, this zip file contains
a fully functional basic pytest script.
Pytest is a thing that tests packages, pytest itself is a package that some-
one might want to install, especially if they're looking to run tests inside
some package they want to install. Pytest has a lot of code to collect and
execute tests, and other such sort of "tribal knowledge" that has been en-
coded in its code base. Because of this we basically include a basic copy
of pytest inside this blob. We do this  because it let's you as a maintainer
or application developer who wants people who don't deal with python much to
easily run tests without installing the complete pytest package.
If you're wondering how this is created: you can create it yourself if you
have a complete pytest installation by using this command on the command-
line: ``py.test --genscript=runtests.py``.

variables.name: runtests.sources
documentation: ensure bytes

functions.name: importmagic.util.parse_ast
documentation: Parse source into a Python AST, taking care of encoding.

functions.name: importmagic.util.dump
documentation:
Return a formatted dump of the tree in *node*.

This is mainly useful for debugging purposes.  The returned string will
show the names and the values for fields.  This makes the code impossible
to evaluate, so if evaluation is wanted *annotate_fields* must be set to
False.  Attributes such as line numbers and column offsets are not dumped
by default.  If this is wanted, *include_attributes* can be set to True.

modules.name: importmagic
documentation:
Python Import Magic - automagically add, remove and manage imports

This module just exports the main API of importmagic.

modules.name: importmagic.six
documentation: Utilities for writing code that runs on Python 2 and 3

functions.name: importmagic.six._add_doc
documentation: Add documentation to a function.

functions.name: importmagic.six._import_module
documentation: Import module, returning the module after the last dot.

functions.name: importmagic.six.add_move
documentation: Add an item to six.moves.

functions.name: importmagic.six.remove_move
documentation: Remove item from six.moves.

functions.name: importmagic.six.iterkeys
documentation: Return an iterator over the keys of a dictionary.

functions.name: importmagic.six.itervalues
documentation: Return an iterator over the values of a dictionary.

functions.name: importmagic.six.iteritems
documentation: Return an iterator over the (key, value) pairs of a dictionary.

functions.name: importmagic.six.iterlists
documentation: Return an iterator over the (key, [values]) pairs of a dictionary.

functions.name: importmagic.six.u
documentation: Workaround for standalone backslash

functions.name: importmagic.six.exec_
documentation: Execute code in a namespace.

functions.name: importmagic.six.print_
documentation: The new-style print function for Python 2.4 and 2.5.

functions.name: importmagic.six.with_metaclass
documentation: Create a base class with a metaclass.

functions.name: importmagic.six.add_metaclass
documentation: Class decorator for creating a class with a metaclass.

classes.name: importmagic.six.X
documentation: It's possible to have sizeof(long) != sizeof(Py_ssize_t).

classes.name: importmagic.six._MovedItems
documentation: Lazy loading of moved objects

classes.name: importmagic.six.Module_six_moves_urllib_parse
documentation: Lazy loading of moved objects in six.moves.urllib_parse

classes.name: importmagic.six.Module_six_moves_urllib_error
documentation: Lazy loading of moved objects in six.moves.urllib_error

classes.name: importmagic.six.Module_six_moves_urllib_request
documentation: Lazy loading of moved objects in six.moves.urllib_request

classes.name: importmagic.six.Module_six_moves_urllib_response
documentation: Lazy loading of moved objects in six.moves.urllib_response

classes.name: importmagic.six.Module_six_moves_urllib_robotparser
documentation: Lazy loading of moved objects in six.moves.urllib_robotparser

classes.name: importmagic.six.Module_six_moves_urllib
documentation: Create a six.moves.urllib namespace that resembles the Python 3 namespace

modules.name: importmagic.importer
documentation: Imports new symbols.

modules.name: importmagic.symbols
documentation: Parse Python source and extract unresolved symbols.

methods.name: importmagic.symbols.Scope.find_unresolved_and_unreferenced_symbols
documentation:
Find any unresolved symbols, and unreferenced symbols from this scope.

:returns: ({unresolved}, {unreferenced})

modules.name: importmagic.index
documentation: Build an index of top-level symbols from Python modules and packages.

methods.name: importmagic.index.SymbolIndex.index_path
documentation:
Index a path.

```
param root

Either a package directory, a .so or a .py module.
```

methods.name: importmagic.index.SymbolIndex.get_or_create_index
documentation: Get index with given name from cache. Create if it doesn't exists.

methods.name: importmagic.index.SymbolIndex.symbol_scores
documentation:
Find matches for symbol.

```
param symbol

A . separated symbol. eg. 'os.path.basename'

returns

A list of tuples of (score, package, reference|None),
ordered by score from highest to lowest.
```

methods.name: importmagic.index.SymbolIndex.find
documentation: Return the node for a path, or None.

methods.name: importmagic.index.SymbolIndex.location_for
documentation: Return the location code for a path.
